$comment: Scenarios are sequences of actions from actions.json that walk the application through interesting paths. Each scenario is a separate pytest case. The runner takes a screenshot at every node marked snapshot:true and verifies state matches expectations after each step.

scenarios.name: tab_lifecycle
scenarios.description: Open a local tab, write to it, close it. Smallest meaningful round-trip.

scenarios.name: view_toggles
scenarios.description: Sidebar + git panel each toggle and return.

scenarios.name: gtk_plugin_hot_toggle
scenarios.description: Disable + re-enable the seeded test_panel GTK plugin without restart.

scenarios.name: sidecar_lifecycle_direct
scenarios.description: Direct sidecar start/stop via /api/sidecars/{name}/{action} (no tab refcount path).

scenarios.name: sidecar_via_tab_refcount
scenarios.description: Per-tab plugin assignment drives sidecar lifecycle. Refcount 0→1 starts, 1→0 stops.

scenarios.name: intro_prompt_per_tab
scenarios.description: Two tabs, one with sleeper enabled, one without. Intro prompts diverge.

scenarios.name: guard_rails
scenarios.description: Negative-path: every guard endpoint refuses correctly.
